title: Configure Memory Limit on Containers via Quadlet
body:
### Feature request description

Currently, Quadlet creates containers with an uncapped memory limit.

Normally when I work with Containers, I limit memory per container as a safeguard against memory leaks (Even if it is improbable).

### Suggest potential solution

A `Memory` argument that can be added under `[Container]` in a Quadlet `.container` file.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Respecting `MemoryMax` as declared for `[Service]`

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
With quadlet, you can manage things like cgroups in the Systemd [service] section rather then manage it via podman.
I believe we should allow systemd to manage the cgroup, it also controls the podman service and any other tools executed.

For example in Quadlet we do.
```
[Service]
AllowedCPUs=1
CPUWeight=50
Delegate=true
IOWeight=50
ManagedOOMSwap=kill
MemorySwapMax=0
# Containers within the qm y default set OOMScoreAdj to 750
OOMScoreAdjust=500
Restart=always
Slice=QM.slice
Environment=ROOTFS=/usr/lib/qm/rootfs

[Container]
AddCapability=all
AddDevice=-/dev/kvm
AddDevice=-/dev/fuse
ContainerName=qm
Exec=/sbin/init
Network=host
PodmanArgs=--security-opt label=nested --security-opt unmask=all
ReadOnly=true
Rootfs=${ROOTFS}
SecurityLabelFileType=qm_file_t
SecurityLabelLevel=s0
SecurityLabelType=qm_t
Timezone=local
Volume=${ROOTFS}/etc:/etc
Volume=${ROOTFS}/var:/var
```

> With quadlet, you can manage things like cgroups in the Systemd [service] section rather then manage it via podman. I believe we should allow systemd to manage the cgroup, it also controls the podman service and any other tools executed.

Perhaps my issue then is with the Cockpit UI. Since I have a unit with a `MemoryMax` of `2G` but it reads as the following (2.08/16.7GB)

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/38189170/d8e0d14c-ad99-4a64-80b1-18292bcd9ffa)

If you must, you could pass those options manually using PodmanArgs:

```ini
[Container]
PodmanArgs=--memory 128m --cpus 0.25
...
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think we should rely on Systemd for setting this up or just use podmanargs to pass it down.  Having both will just confuse things.
Perhaps we should enhance the Quadlet man page to make it easier to discover settings for it.
